title: The First Open s = 5 Case for BSHM(8rs,4s,4s,0)
micro_paper_assessment: Strong structural slice only. The forced 20K6/block-zero-sum reduction is theorem-facing, but there is no exact existence verdict yet, so the current packet is not paper-ready.
family_affinity: high; the same B^2=nB reduction extends across the Type 2 family BSHM(8rs,4s,4s,0)
generalization_signal: The selected-row column graph is forced to be 4s disjoint cliques K_{2r}; equivalently H1 is column-permutation equivalent to H_(4s) ⊗ 1_(2r)^T.
candidate_theorem_slice: Any BSHM(120,20,20,0), if it exists, has selected 20-row submatrix H_20 ⊗ 1_6^T up to column permutation, and every complementary row has a 3+/3- split on each six-column block.
next_action: verify the 20K6 reduction carefully, then decide whether the complementary 100-row block model can support a bounded exact obstruction or extension argument without generic search
